text: Handling deadlines effectively is an important skill in both study and work. Good time management helps you plan your tasks and avoid last-minute stress. Try breaking large projects into smaller, manageable steps with their own mini-deadlines. Prioritization is also key; identify which tasks are most urgent or important, and focus on those first. Scope management means knowing your limits and not taking on too much at once—learn to say no when necessary. Finally, communication is essential. If you see a deadline slipping, talk to your manager or professor early. Most people appreciate honesty and may offer help or extend the deadline. With these strategies, you can stay calm and meet your goals.
